wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Iron Man project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as an immersive, scroll-driven cinematic experience showcasing Iron Man's suit animations. Document the technology stack including Next.js 16, React, TypeScript, Framer Motion, and Lenis smooth scrolling. Describe the three main cinematic experiences: Hero sequence with 169-frame canvas animation, Cinematic reveal with beat markers and dialogue cards, and Systems Nominal with animated telemetry data. Include the project's architectural approach using component-based design with provider patterns. Provide context for target audience, use cases, and the overall visual storytelling goals. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and explain how the different animation systems work together to create the immersive experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Hierarchy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the component hierarchy and organization in the Iron Man project. Detail the Next.js app router structure with RootLayout as the main container, SmoothScrollProvider as the global context provider, and page composition patterns. Document the section-based architecture showing how Hero, CinematicReveal, and SystemsNominal components are structured and composed. Explain the UI component organization including reusable elements like Navbar, HudFrame, EyebrowBadge, and AnimatedSection. Include component relationship diagrams showing parent-child relationships, prop drilling patterns, and data flow between components. Address the modular design approach and how components are organized into sections and UI categories.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hero Canvas Animation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Hero canvas animation system. Explain the 169-frame canvas animation architecture including frame loading with progressive loading indicators, device pixel ratio handling for high-DPI displays, and canvas resizing strategies. Document the scroll-triggered animation system using requestAnimationFrame, progress calculation, and frame index mapping. Cover the dialogue card system with timing-based visibility controls, text fade effects with opacity and transform animations, and HUD element integration. Include the power readout animation, sequence counter display, and loading overlay implementation. Provide practical examples of extending frame assets, modifying animation timing, and customizing dialogue sequences.

wiki_catalogs.name: HUD Frame Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the HudFrame component used for creating custom SVG HUD corner frames. Document the component's props including corner positioning (tl, tr, bl, br), size customization, and className options. Explain the SVG path generation logic for each corner type and how the component renders different HUD corner styles. Include usage examples showing how to integrate HUD frames into the Iron Man interface design. Document styling options, responsive behavior, and accessibility considerations. Provide guidelines for customizing HUD frame appearance and integrating with the overall UI theme.

wiki_catalogs.name: Code Quality Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive code quality standards documentation for the Iron Man project. Document the ESLint configuration including custom rules, plugin integrations, and linting workflows. Explain TypeScript strict mode settings, type safety enforcement, and compilation targets. Cover PostCSS configuration for Tailwind CSS integration, CSS optimization, and build pipeline setup. Include code formatting standards, import organization rules, and naming conventions. Document pre-commit hooks, CI/CD integration, and automated quality checks. Provide guidelines for maintaining consistent code style across animation components, UI elements, and utility modules. Address common TypeScript pitfalls in animation contexts and best practices for type-safe canvas operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Iron Man project. Include detailed installation instructions covering Node.js requirements, package manager options (npm, yarn, pnpm, bun), and step-by-step setup process. Document the development server startup commands and local environment configuration. Explain the project structure basics and how to access the application at http://localhost:3000. Cover initial customization options and basic navigation. Include troubleshooting for common setup issues and environment-specific considerations. Provide guidance on browser compatibility and recommended development tools. Keep the content beginner-friendly while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get up and running.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the data flow patterns throughout the Iron Man animation system. Explain how scroll position data flows from SmoothScrollProvider to animation components, how frame indices are calculated from scroll progress, and how animation states are propagated through the component tree. Document the coordination between different animation systems including canvas frame loading, Framer Motion viewport detection, and HUD element visibility control. Detail the state management patterns for scroll-aware components, animation progress tracking, and component visibility states. Include diagrams showing data flow from user input (scroll events) through calculation layers to visual output (canvas rendering, motion animations). Address performance considerations in the data flow pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cinematic Reveal System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Cinematic Reveal system featuring beat markers and frame-by-frame playback. Detail the three-beat animation architecture, frame sequence management, and HUD element visibility controls. Explain the beat marker system for segmenting the animation timeline, sequence counter implementation showing current frame progression, and dialogue card integration with timing-based visibility. Document the scroll-triggered animation mechanics, progress calculation algorithms, and frame rendering pipeline. Include examples of adding new beat segments, customizing HUD elements, and implementing additional animated telemetry displays.

wiki_catalogs.name: Eyebrow Badge Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the EyebrowBadge component used for displaying status indicators and labels throughout the Iron Man interface. Document the component's visual appearance, styling options, and usage patterns for different badge types. Explain how eyebrow badges integrate with the overall design system and contribute to the HUD aesthetic. Include examples of badge usage in different contexts such as system status indicators, notifications, and informational displays. Document customization options, color schemes, and responsive behavior. Provide guidelines for maintaining visual consistency with the Iron Man theme.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Development Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed component development patterns for the Iron Man project. Document proper prop interface design for animation components, including frame indices, timing parameters, and scroll position handlers. Explain state management patterns for scroll-aware components, animation state machines, and performance optimization techniques. Cover component composition strategies for UI elements like HUD frames, animated sections, and interactive elements. Include guidelines for TypeScript interfaces in animation contexts, event handler patterns, and ref management for canvas elements. Document lifecycle considerations for scroll-triggered animations, cleanup procedures, and memory leak prevention. Provide examples of reusable component patterns and extension points for custom animation sequences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Iron Man project's component-based architecture. Describe the high-level design using Next.js app router with page composition, provider pattern for global state management, and modular component organization. Document the animation system architecture including canvas-based frame sequences, Framer Motion integration, and smooth scrolling implementation. Explain the relationship between sections (Hero, Cinematic Reveal, Systems Nominal), UI components, and utility libraries. Detail the data flow patterns from scroll events to animation triggers, and how different animation systems coordinate. Include component hierarchy diagrams showing relationships between providers, sections, and UI components. Address the design patterns used including Provider pattern, Factory pattern for frame loading, and Observer pattern for scroll events.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the design patterns implemented throughout the Iron Man project. Document the Provider pattern used in SmoothScrollProvider for global state management, the Factory pattern utilized in frame loading systems for animation sequences, and the Observer pattern implemented through Intersection Observer API for scroll-based triggers. Explain the Component composition pattern used for building complex UI elements from smaller reusable components. Detail the viewport-based animation pattern integrating Framer Motion with scroll detection. Include implementation examples from the codebase showing how each pattern is applied, the benefits they provide, and how they contribute to the overall architecture. Address pattern selection rationale and alternatives considered.

wiki_catalogs.name: Systems Nominal Telemetry
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Systems Nominal section's animated telemetry data display system. Explain the animated section transition components, eyebrow badge system implementation, and scroll-triggered animation patterns. Document the telemetry data visualization techniques, animated progress indicators, and interactive elements. Cover the Framer Motion integration for viewport-based animations, section transition effects, and responsive design considerations. Include examples of adding new telemetry data sources, customizing animation timing, and implementing additional animated UI components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animated Section Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AnimatedSection component that provides scroll-triggered Framer Motion animations. Document the component's integration with viewport detection, animation trigger mechanisms, and motion configurations. Explain how the component coordinates with the smooth scrolling system to create seamless scroll-driven animations. Include examples of different animation types, timing configurations, and trigger conditions. Document performance optimization techniques, intersection observer settings, and animation lifecycle management. Provide guidelines for creating custom animated sections and integrating with the overall cinematic experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive animation best practices documentation for the Iron Man project. Document canvas animation optimization techniques including frame buffering, device pixel ratio handling, and render performance tuning. Explain scroll-driven animation patterns using Framer Motion, intersection observer optimization, and requestAnimationFrame best practices. Cover memory management strategies for large frame sequences, canvas context reuse, and garbage collection prevention. Include guidelines for smooth scrolling implementation with Lenis, scroll event throttling, and performance monitoring. Document animation timing functions, easing curves, and frame rate optimization. Provide troubleshooting guides for common animation issues including stuttering, memory leaks, and cross-browser compatibility problems. Address animation debugging techniques and performance profiling tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Iron Man project's animation systems. Detail the three main animation sequences: Hero canvas animation with 169-frame sequences, Cinematic reveal with beat markers and frame-by-frame playback, and Systems Nominal with animated telemetry data. Explain the frame loading architecture, canvas rendering techniques, and device pixel ratio handling for high-DPI displays. Document the scroll-triggered animation system using Intersection Observer API and viewport detection. Cover the dialogue card system, HUD element visibility controls, and sequence counter implementations. Include performance optimization techniques, memory management for large frame assets, and animation timing calculations. Provide practical examples of extending the animation system and adding new sequences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Points
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the integration points and extension mechanisms throughout the Iron Man animation system. Detail how external libraries integrate with the core system including Canvas API integration for frame rendering, Framer Motion viewport detection, and Lenis smooth scrolling implementation. Explain the extension points for adding new animation sequences through the frame loading system, customizing HUD elements via component composition, and extending scroll behaviors through provider customization. Document the integration interfaces between animation libraries and DOM manipulation, the relationship between utility libraries and animation systems, and how UI components integrate with section components. Include guidelines for maintaining integration integrity when extending functionality and best practices for adding new integration points.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Animation Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for the shared animation utility modules that support the main animation systems. Detail the frame path generation functions, animation constants, and shared configuration objects. Explain the dialogue data structures, beat marker definitions, and animation timing constants used across different sections. Document the utility functions for frame asset management, progress calculation helpers, and animation state coordination. Include examples of extending the utility modules, adding new animation constants, and creating reusable animation helpers for future projects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation Bar Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Navbar component responsible for site navigation with scroll-aware styling. Document the component's responsive design implementation, scroll detection mechanisms, and visual state changes based on scroll position. Explain how the navbar integrates with the smooth scrolling system and maintains visual consistency with the Iron Man theme. Include examples of navigation patterns, menu configurations, and interactive elements. Document styling options, accessibility features, and mobile responsiveness. Provide guidelines for customizing navbar appearance and adding new navigation items while maintaining the cinematic design language.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive testing strategies for the Iron Man project's animation and UI components. Document unit testing approaches for animation logic, frame calculation algorithms, and scroll position calculations. Explain integration testing strategies for scroll-triggered animations, component composition, and state management. Cover visual regression testing for animation sequences, HUD elements, and responsive layouts. Include testing methodologies for cross-browser compatibility, mobile responsiveness, and performance benchmarks. Document mocking strategies for canvas operations, scroll events, and animation frames. Provide guidelines for testing animation timing, sequence correctness, and user interaction patterns. Address continuous integration testing, automated performance monitoring, and quality assurance workflows for animation-heavy applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Iron Man project's UI component library. Document each reusable component including HudFrame for custom SVG HUD corners, EyebrowBadge for status indicators, AnimatedSection for scroll-triggered Framer Motion effects, Navbar for navigation with scroll awareness, and Footer for archive navigation. For each component, describe visual appearance, behavior, user interaction patterns, props/attributes, customization options, and integration patterns. Include usage examples with code snippets showing proper implementation. Document component composition patterns, state management, and responsive design considerations. Address accessibility compliance, cross-browser compatibility, and performance optimization. Provide guidelines for extending components and creating new UI elements that follow the project's design language.

wiki_catalogs.name: Footer Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Footer component that provides archive navigation and site information display. Document the component's layout structure, navigation patterns, and integration with the overall site architecture. Explain how the footer contributes to the user experience and provides access to archived content. Include examples of footer content organization, link structures, and interactive elements. Document styling considerations, responsive behavior, and accessibility compliance. Provide guidelines for customizing footer content and maintaining consistency with the Iron Man interface design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for the Iron Man project. Document TypeScript configuration, ESLint rules, and PostCSS setup for consistent code quality. Explain component development patterns including proper prop interfaces, state management, and lifecycle considerations. Cover animation best practices for canvas rendering, scroll performance optimization, and memory management. Document code organization standards, file naming conventions, and component structure guidelines. Include testing strategies for animation components, scroll behavior verification, and cross-browser compatibility testing. Address performance profiling techniques, bundle size optimization, and production deployment considerations. Provide guidelines for extending the animation system, adding new UI components, and maintaining code quality across the project.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed performance optimization documentation for the Iron Man project. Document canvas rendering optimization techniques including device pixel ratio handling, frame buffer management, and memory cleanup strategies. Explain scroll performance optimization using Lenis integration, requestAnimationFrame usage, and intersection observer efficiency. Cover animation performance best practices including frame rate optimization, GPU acceleration, and resource loading strategies. Document Next.js specific optimizations including static generation, image optimization, and code splitting. Include profiling techniques for identifying performance bottlenecks, memory usage analysis, and bundle size optimization. Address mobile performance considerations, battery life impact, and throttling strategies. Provide practical examples of implementing performance improvements and measuring their impact.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Iron Man Website - Project Overview

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Iron Man is a visually stunning interactive website showcasing the iconic Marvel character Tony Stark's Iron Man suit through an immersive scrolling experience. The project serves as both a digital tribute to the franchise and a demonstration of modern web technologies for creating cinematic web experiences.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Create an engaging, scroll-driven narrative experience that tells the story of Iron Man's evolution
- Demonstrate advanced web animation techniques including canvas-based frame sequences and smooth scrolling
- Showcase cutting-edge React patterns with Next.js 16 and TypeScript
- Implement sophisticated UI components with Tailwind CSS styling and custom animations

### Target Audience
- Marvel fans and pop culture enthusiasts
- Web developers interested in advanced frontend techniques
- Designers exploring scroll-based storytelling
- Tech professionals learning modern React patterns

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a modular React architecture built on Next.js 16 with TypeScript:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Root Layout] --> B[SmoothScrollProvider]
    B --> C[Main Page]
    C --> D[Navbar]
    C --> E[Hero Section]
    C --> F[Cinematic Reveal]
    C --> G[Systems Nominal]
    C --> H[Footer]
    
    E --> I[Canvas Animation]
    E --> J[Hud Frames]
    E --> K[Eyebrow Badges]
    
    F --> L[Second Canvas Animation]
    F --> M[Beat Cards]
    
    G --> N[Telemetry Data]
    G --> O[Animated Sections]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Provider Pattern**: SmoothScrollProvider manages global scroll behavior
- **Section-Based Architecture**: Each major screen area is implemented as a separate section component
- **Canvas Animation Pattern**: Custom frame-by-frame animation system using HTML5 Canvas
- **Viewport-Based Animation**: Framer Motion integration with Intersection Observer for scroll-triggered animations

### System Relationships
The application uses a hierarchical component structure where the root layout provides global context, while individual sections manage their own state and animations independently.

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Root Layout** (`src/app/layout.tsx`): Sets up global fonts, metadata, and smooth scrolling provider
- **Home Page** (`src/app/page.tsx`): Orchestrates the main page composition with all sections

### Core Modules
- **Smooth Scroll Provider** (`src/components/providers/SmoothScrollProvider.tsx`): Implements Lenis smooth scrolling with requestAnimationFrame optimization
- **Hero Section** (`src/components/sections/Hero.tsx`): Complex canvas-based animation with 169-frame sequence
- **Cinematic Reveal** (`src/components/sections/CinematicReveal.tsx`): Second canvas animation with beat markers and dialogue cards
- **Animation System** (`src/components/ui/AnimatedSection.tsx`): Framer Motion integration for scroll-triggered animations

### Configuration Approach
- **Next.js Configuration** (`next.config.ts`): Optimized for static generation and modern React features
- **TypeScript Configuration** (`tsconfig.json`): Strict typing with modern ECMAScript features
- **Tailwind CSS** (`postcss.config.mjs`): Utility-first styling with custom theme variables

### External Dependencies
- **React 19.2.4**: Latest React with concurrent features
- **Next.js 16.2.2**: Production-ready React framework
- **Framer Motion 12.38.0**: Advanced animation library
- **Lenis 1.3.21**: Smooth scrolling implementation
- **Geist Fonts**: Modern font family with CSS variables
- **Phosphor Icons**: SVG icon library

### Integration Points
- **Canvas API**: Direct pixel manipulation for frame-based animations
- **Intersection Observer**: Scroll-based animation triggers
- **Device Pixel Ratio**: High-DPI canvas rendering optimization
- **CSS Variables**: Dynamic theming and responsive design

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[SmoothScrollProvider] --> B[Lenis Engine]
    B --> C[RequestAnimationFrame]
    C --> D[Scroll Events]
    
    E[Hero Section] --> F[Canvas Context]
    F --> G[Frame Loading]
    G --> H[Animation Loop]
    
    I[AnimatedSection] --> J[Framer Motion]
    J --> K[Viewport Detection]
    K --> L[Spring Animations]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The website implements three primary cinematic experiences:

1. **Hero Sequence**: 169-frame canvas animation with dynamic HUD elements
2. **Cinematic Reveal**: Frame-by-frame playback of significant Iron Man moments
3. **System Diagnostics**: Interactive telemetry display with animated entries

### Implementation Highlights

#### Canvas Animation System
- **Frame Loading**: Efficient preloading of sequential image frames
- **Dynamic Resizing**: Device pixel ratio aware canvas scaling
- **Performance Optimization**: requestAnimationFrame-based animation loops
- **Responsive Design**: Adaptive canvas sizing for different screen sizes

#### Scroll-Triggered Animations
- **Viewport Detection**: Intersection Observer API for scroll-based triggers
- **Spring Physics**: Natural motion curves using Framer Motion
- **Staggered Effects**: Sequential animations with configurable delays

#### HUD Interface Elements
- **Custom SVG Components**: Modular HUD frame system with corner positioning
- **Dynamic Telemetry**: Real-time power level simulation with sine wave calculations
- **Responsive Typography**: Fluid typography scaling across device sizes

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> FramePreloading: Images Loaded
    FramePreloading --> CanvasInitialization: Ready
    CanvasInitialization --> AnimationLoop: Start
    AnimationLoop --> ScrollInteraction: User Scrolls
    ScrollInteraction --> FrameUpdate: Progress Changes
    FrameUpdate --> AnimationLoop: Continue
    AnimationLoop --> TelemetryUpdate: Power Readout
    TelemetryUpdate --> AnimationLoop: Update Complete
    AnimationLoop --> [*]: Page Exit
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
Iron Man demonstrates a sophisticated blend of modern web technologies, combining Next.js server-side rendering with client-side canvas animations and advanced React patterns. The project successfully balances performance optimization with rich visual storytelling.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Modular Component Design**: Clean separation between sections and reusable UI components
- **Performance Optimization**: Efficient canvas rendering and smooth scrolling implementation
- **Modern Tooling**: Up-to-date React ecosystem with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
- **Responsive Architecture**: Adaptive design patterns for various screen sizes

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Code Splitting**: Consider lazy-loading heavy canvas components for better initial load times
- **Accessibility**: Enhanced ARIA labels for canvas elements and improved keyboard navigation
- **Testing Strategy**: Implement unit tests for animation logic and component interactions
- **Performance Monitoring**: Add metrics collection for animation frame rates and memory usage

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Lazy Loading**: Use React.lazy for non-critical sections to improve initial page load
2. **Add Accessibility Features**: Implement proper ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation support
3. **Create Performance Benchmarks**: Establish baseline metrics for animation performance across devices
4. **Expand Testing Coverage**: Add Storybook stories and unit tests for complex animation logic

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Mobile Optimization**: Further refine canvas performance on lower-powered devices
- **Interactive Elements**: Add clickable elements within HUD displays for enhanced engagement
- **Content Management**: Implement a CMS for easy updates to quotes and telemetry data
- **Analytics Integration**: Add performance monitoring and user interaction analytics

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [layout.tsx](src/app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx)
- [Hero.tsx](src/components/sections/Hero.tsx)
- [CinematicReveal.tsx](src/components/sections/CinematicReveal.tsx)
- [SystemsNominal.tsx](src/components/sections/SystemsNominal.tsx)
- [SmoothScrollProvider.tsx](src/components/providers/SmoothScrollProvider.tsx)
- [AnimatedSection.tsx](src/components/ui/AnimatedSection.tsx)
- [Navbar.tsx](src/components/ui/Navbar.tsx)
- [HudFrame.tsx](src/components/ui/HudFrame.tsx)
- [Footer.tsx](src/components/sections/Footer.tsx)
- [hero.ts](src/lib/hero.ts)
- [cinematic.ts](src/lib/cinematic.ts)

</blog>
